language_reference.name: JSONata
language_reference.description: A lightweight query and transformation language for JSON data. It allows querying, filtering, and transforming JSON structures into new outputs.

context.description: Represents the current context or the root of the input JSON document. Useful when navigating up a hierarchy or starting an absolute path.

path_navigation.description: Used to navigate through JSON objects. E.g., 'Address.City' accesses the City property inside the Address object.

variables.description: Variables start with a dollar sign. They can be bound to values using the ':=' operator. E.g., '$myVar := 5'.

path_operators.name: Predicate
path_operators.description: Filters an array based on a condition. E.g., 'Phone[type="mobile"]' returns only phone objects where type is mobile.

path_operators.name: Order-by / Sort
path_operators.description: Sorts an array. Use '>' for descending and '<' for ascending. E.g., 'Account.Order^(>Price)'.

path_operators.name: Descendant wildcard
path_operators.description: Recursively searches all descendants for a property name. E.g., '**.*' finds all properties at any depth.

path_operators.name: Wildcard
path_operators.description: Matches any property name at the current level.

comparison_operators.description: Checks if a value exists in an array. E.g., 'status in ["active", "pending"]'

conditional_logic.description: Ternary operator for conditional evaluation. The ': false_expression' part is optional.

regular_expressions.description: Regex literals are enclosed in forward slashes. Supported flags include 'i' (case-insensitive) and 'm' (multiline). E.g., '/hello/i'.

object.description: Creates a new JSON object. Keys and values are separated by colons. E.g., '{ "Name": FullName, "Age": 30 }'.

grouping.description: Objects can be grouped using curly braces inside a step. E.g., 'Account.Order { ProductID: Price }'.

chaining.description: Function chaining (pipe) operator. Passes the result of the left side as the first argument to the function on the right. E.g., 'Account.Email ~> $substringBefore("@")'.

blocks.description: Groups multiple expressions separated by semicolons. Returns the result of the last evaluated expression. E.g., '($a := 1; $b := 2; $a + $b)'.

lambda_functions.description: Anonymous functions (lambdas) can be assigned to variables and passed as arguments. E.g., '$square := function($n) { $n * $n }'.

string_functions.description: Returns the substring before the first occurrence of 'chars'.
Returns the substring after the first occurrence of 'chars'.

numeric_functions.description: Returns the sum of an array of numbers.
Returns the maximum number in an array.
Returns the minimum number in an array.

array_functions.description: Returns the number of items in an array.
Reverses the order of items in an array.
Returns an array containing the unique values from the input array.

object_functions.description: Returns an array of the keys in an object.
Returns the value associated with the key in the object.
Merges an array of objects into a single object.

datetime_functions.description: Returns the current UTC timestamp in ISO 8601 string format.
Returns the current UTC timestamp as milliseconds since the Unix epoch.
Formats a millisecond timestamp into a string based on an optional picture string and timezone.
Parses a timestamp string into milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

regex_functions.description: Applies a regex pattern to a string and returns an array of objects representing the matches.
Finds pattern matches in a string and replaces them with the replacement string or function.

higher_order_functions.description: Applies a function to each item in an array and returns an array of the results.
Returns an array containing only the items in the input array for which the function returns true.
Applies a reducer function to each element of the array, resulting in a single output value.
Returns an object containing only the key/value pairs from the input object for which the function returns true.
